text: Increasing numbers of modern scientific experiments involve the synchronization of many different signals derived from varying modalities and types of recording devices. Due to the sequential nature of program code, current approaches typically are unable to accurately represent and utilize the true parallel nature of the signals present in many real-time data acquisitions. We have developed an application called Bonsai, a high performance, modular, open source graphical programming language framework for gathering and processing multiple sources of different types of data in real time. This paper discusses in more detail its general architecture and provides several examples of the application of this tool for experimentation in neuroscience where Bonsai was used to manage and integrate various sensor devices and systems including video based behavioral analysis of rodents, distributed neurophysiology, and closed loop brain stimulation.
